title: Slice lifecycle safety
goal: Prevent accidental historical dependency reruns by giving JSON Issue Slices an explicit open/closed lifecycle and treating closed dependencies as already satisfied.
acceptance: Slice JSON supports an issue-style lifecycle where omitted status means open and status=closed records accepted historical work.
Successful daemon runs close completed slice JSON with closed_by_run and closed_at before final reports are written.
run --slice skips closed dependencies instead of launching historical workers again.
run --slice rejects an explicitly requested closed slice and tells the operator to create a follow-up slice.
Existing historical slices in this repository are marked closed so future release-polish work cannot accidentally rerun them.
Regression tests cover closed dependency skipping, explicit closed-slice rejection, and automatic closing after a successful run.
must_ask_if: The implementation would rerun closed historical slices by default.
The implementation would silently reopen a closed slice.
The implementation would remove JSON Issue Slices as the machine source of truth.
